items.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
items.why_it_matters: Microsoft is discontinuing Claude Code licenses. You rely on Claude Code for your development, so this change means you'll need to find alternative tools or adjust your workflow soon. This week, research alternatives to Claude Code like other AI coding assistants or explore how to integrate Claude's API directly into your IDE for similar functionality.

items.title: Show HN: Neuz, a self-hosted news dashboard curated by Claude
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer shipped "Neuz," a self-hosted news dashboard that uses Claude for curation. This is a direct example of someone shipping a project very similar to your AI-ingest tool, using Claude. You can learn from their approach to building and deploying. This week, review the Neuz GitHub repo to see how they structured their Claude integration for news curation and consider adapting any useful patterns for your own AI-ingest project.

items.title: Launch HN: Superset (YC P26) – IDE for the agents era
items.why_it_matters: Superset, an open-source IDE, launched to help developers run multiple coding agents like Claude Code and Codex in parallel. This tool is designed to improve the "direction" part of your vibe coding by letting you manage and run several AI coding agents simultaneously, potentially speeding up your development and pushing you to ship faster. This week, try installing Superset and experimenting with running Claude Code or Codex agents in parallel on a small task for your AI-ingest project to see if it improves your workflow efficiency.

items.title: Deep codebase context cuts Claude Code's token cost by 47%
items.why_it_matters: New techniques for providing deep codebase context to Claude Code can significantly reduce token costs. You use Claude Code daily, and managing token costs is important. Learning these techniques could save you money and make your AI-driven development more efficient. This week, read the article to understand how "deep codebase context" is applied and try implementing one of their suggested methods in your current Claude Code workflow to see if you can reduce your token usage.

items.title: Show HN: Myco – coordinate Claude and DeepSeek and other LLMs in one agent swarm
items.why_it_matters: Myco is a new open-source tool that allows you to coordinate multiple LLMs like Claude and DeepSeek within a single agent swarm. This aligns with your interest in delegating coding tasks to different LLMs for cost savings and leveraging specific model strengths, enhancing your "direction" skills for complex AI workflows. This week, explore Myco's documentation and examples to understand how it orchestrates different LLMs, then consider setting up a basic agent swarm to handle a small, specific coding task for your AI-ingest project using both Claude and a cheaper model like DeepSeek.
